rationale: Review 1 catches three material proof-quality issues that Review 2 misses entirely: the brittle `ext x; induction; simp` patterns in `map_id` (line 98) and `map_comp` (line 120) which hide non-obvious bialgebra-hom coercions behind automation, and the undocumented complex `simp` chain in `comul_includeRight` (line 183) driving Sweedler/tensor-comm rewrites silently. Both reviews correctly flag the `change` redundancy in `comul_includeRight`, but only Review 1 identifies the broader structural brittleness, so acting on Review 1 yields a more robust and maintainable PR.
